skill.name: resubmission-deadline-tracker
skill.description: Track manuscript resubmission deadlines and automatically generate phase-appropriate task breakdowns for academic researchers based on remaining time.
skill.summary: Track manuscript resubmission deadlines. Calculates remaining time, assigns urgency level, and generates task schedules for academic revision workflows.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: 45 static pattern findings were evaluated. All 25 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are false positives triggered by YAML frontmatter separators, markdown formatting, and JSON field labels. All 17 'external command' flags are false positives triggered by bash commands in markdown code blocks (documentation only, not execution). All 6 'system reconnaissance' flags are false positives triggered by print statements and import lines. The 1 'filesystem' flag (main.py:289) is a true positive but represents a legitimate local file write to persistence data within the project directory. No prompt injection or malicious intent was detected. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Local filesystem write for data persistence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: python file write at scripts/main.py:289 writes deadline data to a local JSON file in the data/ directory. This is legitimate behavior for a deadline tracking tool that persists user data locally. No user-controlled path traversal or external data exfiltration. Confidence: 0.95. The file path is hardcoded and contained within the project directory.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub URL in documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: network pattern detected at SKILL.md:7 is a GitHub source attribution link in a markdown blockquote. This is a benign documentation reference, not a network call. No runtime network activity occurs. Confidence: 0.98.

low_findings.title: Shell commands in markdown code blocks
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Multiple backtick patterns detected across SKILL.md and POLISH_CHANGELOG.md. All instances are bash commands inside markdown code fences (```bash ... ```) for documentation and usage examples. These are not executed at runtime. Confidence: 0.99.

content.user_title: Track Resubmission Deadlines with Task Schedules
content.value_statement: Academic researchers struggle to manage manuscript resubmission deadlines while addressing reviewer comments. This skill calculates remaining time, assigns a context-aware urgency level, and generates a phase-appropriate task breakdown for each revision.
actual_capabilities: Calculate remaining time until manuscript resubmission deadline
Assign urgency level based on days remaining (standard, urgent, or emergency)
Generate phase-appropriate task breakdowns with daily targets and checklists
Track multiple manuscript deadlines across different journals in one session
Update progress on tracked manuscripts and adjust remaining task recommendations
Provide actionable recommendations matched to the urgency level
limitations: Does not sync with journal submission systems or send automated reminders
Cannot manage grant, conference, or non-manuscript deadlines
Does not read reviewer comments from files; requires manual issue count input
Default timezone is Asia/Shanghai; users in other regions must specify --timezone to avoid miscalculation

use_cases.title: Track multiple manuscripts under revision
use_cases.description: A researcher juggling revisions for three different journals adds each deadline, views them sorted by urgency, and plans their work queue accordingly.
use_cases.target_user: Active researcher with multiple concurrent manuscript revisions

use_cases.title: Emergency revision with tight deadline
use_cases.description: A co-author discovers a major revision is due in 4 days. The skill triggers urgent mode, identifies must-fix items, and generates a triage schedule for the team.
use_cases.target_user: Researcher facing a fast-approaching resubmission deadline

use_cases.title: First-time resubmission planning
use_cases.description: An early-career researcher receives her first major revision. The skill generates a structured 4-phase plan showing exactly what to do each week.
use_cases.target_user: Early-career researcher new to the revision process

prompt_templates.title: Track a single manuscript revision
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a new resubmission deadline for my paper titled 'The Role of AI in Cancer Detection' for the journal Nature Medicine. The deadline is 2024-08-15. There are 3 major issues and 5 minor issues from the reviewers.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a single revision to track and needs a complete task breakdown

prompt_templates.title: View and compare multiple deadlines
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all my tracked manuscript deadlines sorted by urgency. I have papers under revision at Nature, Science, and PLOS ONE.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has multiple active revisions and needs an overview to prioritize work

prompt_templates.title: Emergency mode triage schedule
prompt_templates.prompt: My Cell paper revision is due in 4 days and I have 5 major reviewer concerns to address. Generate an urgent task schedule that prioritizes the critical fixes.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has very little time remaining and needs a minimal viable revision plan

prompt_templates.title: Update progress and reassess timeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the progress on my Nature Medicine revision to 60% complete and regenerate the task breakdown for the remaining work.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has made partial progress and needs adjusted recommendations for the remaining tasks

output_examples.input: Add a new resubmission deadline for my paper in Nature Medicine. The deadline is 2024-08-15 with 2 major issues and 8 minor issues.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a new deadline entry, calculates 118 days remaining (STANDARD urgency), and generates a 4-phase revision schedule. Phase 1 covers reviewer comment analysis and response strategy. Phase 2 focuses on core revisions including addressing major concerns and updating figures. Phase 3 handles response letter drafting and manuscript polishing. Phase 4 provides buffer time for co-author review and final submission prep.

output_examples.input: My deadline is in 2 days and I still have 5 major issues to fix.
output_examples.output: The skill triggers EMERGENCY mode and calculates less than 3 days remaining. It recommends requesting a deadline extension from the journal editor as the first priority. The task breakdown lists only deal-breaker issues to fix, skips all non-critical improvements, and produces a minimal viable submission plan. It advises submitting even an imperfect revision rather than missing the deadline entirely.

output_examples.input: Show me all my active deadlines and their current status.
output_examples.output: The skill displays a formatted table listing all tracked manuscripts with journal names, deadline dates, remaining time with color-coded urgency emoji indicators (green for relaxed, yellow for standard, blue for active, orange for urgent, red for emergency), and current progress percentages for each entry.

best_practices: Specify your timezone with the --timezone parameter to ensure accurate deadline calculations, especially if you are outside the Asia/Shanghai default zone.
Update your progress percentage regularly so the skill can adjust task recommendations to match your actual completion status.
Enter accurate counts of major and minor reviewer issues to help the skill prioritize the right amount of work for each phase.
anti_patterns: Using this skill to manage grant, conference, or funding deadlines instead of manuscript resubmissions will produce incorrect urgency assessments.
Expecting the skill to read reviewer comments from uploaded PDF files or documents requires manual issue count input instead.
Forgetting to specify a non-default timezone when working outside Asia/Shanghai may cause deadline calculations that are off by several hours.

faq.question: What types of deadlines can I track with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for manuscript resubmission deadlines only. It does not support grant applications, conference submissions, or other academic deadline types.

faq.question: Which timezone does the skill use by default?
faq.answer: The default timezone is Asia/Shanghai. Use the --timezone parameter to specify your local timezone, for example America/New_York or Europe/London.

faq.question: How are urgency levels calculated?
faq.answer: More than 14 days remaining is standard mode with a full 4-phase schedule. Between 3 and 14 days is urgent mode with triage and priority-only execution. Less than 3 days is emergency mode with minimal viable changes.

faq.question: Can I track deadlines for multiple manuscripts at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add unlimited manuscripts, list all active deadlines sorted by urgency, and view individual task breakdowns for each one.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to journal submission portals or send emails?
faq.answer: No. The skill operates entirely locally. It does not sync with any external system, send automated reminders, or connect to journal submission portals.

faq.question: What happens if my deadline has already passed?
faq.answer: The skill detects overdue status and recommends contacting the journal editor immediately to request an extension with a specific new timeline.
